mechanism_narrative: A sustained 3-month decline in the federal funds rate signals an easing monetary cycle that compresses discount rates applied to equity cash flows, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 5-month increase in industrial production signals a strengthening supply chain that boosts corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of rising industrial production are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month rise in the unemployment rate signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of rising unemployment are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month decline in real disposable personal income signals a weakening consumer demand that compresses retail sales, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of declining real disposable personal income are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the federal funds rate above 4.5% signals a tightening monetary cycle that compresses equity valuations, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of rising federal funds rate above 4.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month increase in industrial production and a 2-month rise in housing starts signals a strengthening supply chain and a boost to corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns in periods of rising industrial production and housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 2-month rise in the unemployment rate above 5.5% signals a tightening labor market that compresses wage growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns in periods of rising unemployment above 5.5% are not significantly lower than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A 3-month decline in real disposable personal income and a 2-month decline in housing starts signals a weakening consumer demand and a boost to corporate profitability, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns in periods of declining real disposable personal income and housing starts are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).
